title: Drop Dialysis Protocol
author: Ken Christensen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bc53iy8n

steps.title: Pour 30–100 ml of dialysis buffer,...
steps.instruction: Pour 30–100 ml of dialysis buffer, usually double-distilled water or 1X TE (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0), into a Petri dish or beaker.

steps.title: Float a 13mm or 25 mm...
steps.instruction: Float a 13mm or 25 mm diameter, Type-VS Millipore membrane (MF type, VS filter, mean pore size = 0.025 μm, Millipore, Inc. #VSWP02500 or #VSWP01300) shiny side up on the dialysis buffer. Allow the floating filter to wet completely (~5 minutes) before proceeding. Make sure there are no air bubbles trapped under the filter.

steps.title: Pipette a few μl of the...
steps.instruction: Pipette a few μl of the DNA droplet carefully onto the filter. Depending on the size of the filter, you can dialyze several samples at the same time. Notes If the sample has too much phenol or chloroform, the drop will not remain in the center of the membrane and the dialysis should be discontinued until the organics are further removed. In most cases, this is performed by alcohol precipitation of the sample. If the test sample remains in the center of the membrane, pipette the remainder on to the membrane. Step 3 may be tricky for those with shaky hands or poor hand-eye coordination. The filter has a tendency to move briskly around the surface as you touch it with the pipette tip. Practice with buffer droplets to master the technique before using a valuable sample. Dialysis against double-distilled water is also recommended, especially if proceeding to another manipulation where EDTA might be a problem. Steps 2 to 4 can be repeated with fresh buffer or for longer times if additional dialysis is required.

steps.title: Cover the Petri dish or beaker...
steps.instruction: Cover the Petri dish or beaker and dialyze for 15 minutes to 4 hours. Do not allow the sample to flip or become covered with dialysis buffer.

steps.title: Carefully retrieve the DNA droplet with...
steps.instruction: Carefully retrieve the DNA droplet with a micro-pipette and place it in a microcentrifuge tube. Rinse the spot on the membrane with 50 μl of water or 1X TE (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0) and add to the microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Estimate the concentration of the DNA...
steps.instruction: Estimate the concentration of the DNA product using agarose gel electrophoresis or a spectrophotometer.
